wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the iTip project overview section focused on this macOS menu bar application. Explain its purpose as an automatic application usage tracker that provides quick access to frequently used applications through the menu bar. Document the core functionality including real-time application activation monitoring, usage statistics collection, intelligent ranking algorithms, and one-click application launching. Describe the target audience, key benefits, and primary use cases. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners new to macOS development and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the Swift and AppKit frameworks. Provide practical examples demonstrating common workflows like application switching and usage discovery. Document the application architecture, system requirements (Xcode 16+, macOS 14+), and integration with macOS ecosystem features like Spotlight and NSWorkspace.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system overview documentation for iTip's macOS architecture. Explain the overall system design philosophy centered around the AppDelegate as the central orchestrator managing all subsystems. Document the modular component architecture with clear separation of concerns between data collection, storage, presentation, and application management layers. Describe the observer pattern implementation for system event monitoring, strategy pattern for storage abstraction via UsageStoreProtocol, and factory pattern for menu item creation. Include high-level component interaction diagrams showing the data flow from NSWorkspace application activation events through ActivationMonitor to UsageStore, then to UsageRanker and MenuPresenter for UI display. Address the singleton pattern usage for shared resources and the role of each major component in the system ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: UsageStoreProtocol
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the UsageStoreProtocol interface. Document the protocol contract including the load() method for retrieving usage records and save() method for persisting records. Explain the method signatures, parameter types, return values, and throwing mechanisms. Detail the UsageRecord data structure that flows through this protocol, including field definitions and data types. Provide concrete implementation examples showing how to conform to this protocol, including error handling patterns and thread safety considerations. Document the relationship between UsageStoreProtocol and concrete implementations like UsageStore. Include usage scenarios for loading existing data, saving new records, and handling persistence errors. Address performance considerations and best practices for implementing custom storage backends.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Lifecycle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for iTip's application lifecycle management system. Detail the AppDelegate's role as the central orchestrator, including component initialization sequence, application startup procedures, and shutdown handling. Document the main.swift entry point and its integration with the AppDelegate. Explain the application's initialization flow, component wiring, and dependency injection patterns. Cover application state management, background processing capabilities, and system integration points. Include code examples showing proper initialization sequences, error handling during startup, and graceful shutdown procedures. Address memory management considerations, resource cleanup, and application termination hooks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Activation Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Application Activation Monitoring component. Explain the ActivationMonitor's implementation of NSWorkspace integration for tracking application activation events through the NSWorkspace.didActivateApplicationNotification notification. Document the event handling mechanism, including how the component processes activation notifications, extracts application metadata, and updates usage records. Detail the memory management strategies including the in-memory cache system for avoiding frequent disk I/O operations, the debouncing timer for periodic saves, and the isDirty flag for change tracking. Explain the foreground application tracking logic that calculates active duration for previously active applications. Cover the initialization parameters including store dependency injection, notification center configuration, date provider customization, and self-bundle identifier filtering. Document the startMonitoring and stopMonitoring lifecycle methods, including observer registration and cleanup procedures. Include practical examples of proper usage, error handling for missing application metadata, and troubleshooting common monitoring issues such as permission requirements and notification delivery failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: StatusBarController
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the StatusBarController component responsible for macOS menu bar integration. Document the NSStatusItem initialization, SF Symbol integration for the menu bar icon, and template image handling. Explain the dual initialization pattern supporting both production and testing environments. Detail the NSMenuDelegate implementation including the menuNeedsUpdate method for dynamic menu refresh. Cover the status item lifecycle management, memory cleanup in deinit, and the relationship with MenuPresenter for menu construction. Include implementation details for button configuration, title application, and fallback handling when system symbols are unavailable. Document the integration with NSStatusBar system and the delegation pattern for menu updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Launcher
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Application Launcher component. Thoroughly explain the AppLauncher implementation for managing application lifecycle operations, including running application detection using NSRunningApplication, launch configuration with NSWorkspace.OpenConfiguration, and error handling for application launch failures. Document the AppLaunchError enum structure covering applicationNotFound and launchFailed cases with their respective parameters. Explain the activation workflow that checks for existing running instances before attempting to launch new ones. Cover the completion handler pattern with main thread dispatching and asynchronous operation handling. Include practical examples demonstrating proper usage of the activate method with bundle identifiers. Address common launch issues such as missing applications, permission problems, and system-level failures. Provide troubleshooting guidance for debugging launch operations and handling edge cases in application management workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: UsageStore Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the UsageStore class implementation. Explain the thread-safe architecture using GCD dispatch queues, the caching mechanism for performance optimization, and the JSON serialization/deserialization process. Document the storage URL configuration, file I/O operations, and atomic write operations. Cover the load() method's error handling, fallback mechanisms, and cache invalidation strategies. Detail the save() method's directory creation logic, data encoding, and concurrent access protection. Include practical examples of proper instantiation, usage patterns, and integration with other components. Address thread safety considerations, memory management, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for iTip installation and initial setup. Document the complete installation process including downloading from GitHub Actions, extracting the double-zipped artifact, moving to Applications folder using ditto command, clearing quarantine attributes with xattr, and opening the application. Explain the critical step of moving from Downloads to Applications due to macOS App Translocation protection. Address the 'cannot be verified' developer warning and provide step-by-step resolution. Include system requirements (Xcode 16+, macOS 14+) and build instructions using xcodebuild. Document the initial permission setup for application monitoring and Spotlight integration. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common installation issues and verify successful installation through menu bar icon visibility and basic functionality testing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for component interaction patterns within iTip's architecture. Explain the delegation patterns between StatusBarController and MenuPresenter, the observer relationships in the data collection layer, and the dependency injection approach used by AppDelegate for component orchestration. Document the thread-safe communication patterns between components, including GCD usage for asynchronous operations and weak reference patterns for preventing retain cycles. Detail the event-driven architecture where NSWorkspace triggers ActivationMonitor, which updates UsageStore, triggering MenuPresenter to refresh the status bar menu. Include sequence diagrams showing typical user workflows from application activation to menu updates, and explain error propagation and recovery mechanisms across component boundaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: AppLauncher API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the AppLauncher component's public interface. Document the application launching methods, including launch configuration options and execution parameters. Detail the AppLaunchError enumeration and its error cases, explaining when and how these errors occur during application launch attempts. Provide method signatures, parameter descriptions, and return value specifications. Include practical examples of launching applications by bundle identifier, path, or name. Document error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and fallback options. Explain integration patterns with NSWorkspace and NSRunningApplication frameworks. Address common launch failures, permission issues, and troubleshooting approaches. Include best practices for robust application launching and user experience considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Collection Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for iTip's data collection layer responsible for gathering application usage metrics. Detail the ActivationMonitor's implementation of NSWorkspace integration for tracking application activation events, including event handling mechanisms, memory management strategies, and caching optimizations. Document the NetworkTracker's system command integration for per-process network usage sampling, data parsing algorithms, and aggregation mechanisms. Explain the data collection timing, sampling intervals, and performance considerations. Cover error handling for system-level operations, permission requirements, and fallback mechanisms. Include implementation details for both components, configuration options, and integration patterns with the UsageStore. Provide code examples demonstrating proper usage and troubleshooting common collection issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Usage Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Network Usage Tracking component. Explain the NetworkTracker's system command integration for per-process network usage sampling, including the execution of external commands to gather network statistics. Document the data parsing algorithms used to extract network metrics from system outputs, including byte counters, connection counts, and bandwidth measurements. Detail the aggregation mechanisms that combine raw network data with application usage records to provide comprehensive analytics. Explain the sampling methodology, including timing intervals, data collection frequency, and performance considerations for real-time network monitoring. Cover the error handling strategies for system command failures, permission requirements for network monitoring, and fallback mechanisms when network data is unavailable. Document the integration patterns with the UsageStore for persisting network analytics alongside application usage data. Include implementation examples showing proper initialization, data retrieval workflows, and troubleshooting network monitoring issues such as insufficient privileges and system command execution problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: MenuPresenter
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MenuPresenter component responsible for dynamic menu construction and application display logic. Document the menu building process, including application item creation, icon caching mechanisms, and real-time menu updates. Explain the integration with UsageRanker for application ranking and the display formatting logic. Detail the menu item configuration, including target-action patterns for application launching, keyboard shortcuts, and contextual information display. Cover the relationship with UsageStore for data access and the delegate pattern for menu interaction handling. Include implementation details for menu styling, localization support, and accessibility compliance. Document error handling for missing applications, performance optimization for large application lists, and the refresh cycle for maintaining menu relevance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Spotlight Data Seeder
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Spotlight Data Seeder component. Thoroughly explain the Spotlight framework integration for initial data seeding, including metadata query implementation using Spotlight APIs, data transformation processes from Spotlight results to internal usage records, and performance optimization techniques for efficient data retrieval. Document the Spotlight search mechanisms, query construction, and result processing workflows. Explain how the seeder identifies system applications, extracts relevant metadata, and converts it into the application usage format. Cover performance considerations including query optimization, memory management, and background processing strategies. Include implementation details for handling Spotlight framework limitations, search result filtering, and data consistency validation. Provide examples of Spotlight query patterns and data transformation logic. Address potential issues with Spotlight indexing, permission requirements, and fallback mechanisms when Spotlight data is unavailable.

wiki_catalogs.name: UsageRecord Data Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the UsageRecord data model structure. Detail all field definitions including application identifiers, activation counts, network usage metrics, timestamps, and metadata fields. Document the Codable conformance, JSON serialization format, and field mapping requirements. Explain backward compatibility considerations, optional field handling, and data validation rules. Cover the record lifecycle from creation through updates, including automatic timestamp management and metric accumulation. Provide examples of record creation, modification, and comparison operations. Address data integrity checks, migration strategies for schema changes, and performance considerations for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture & Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for iTip's macOS application design. Describe the high-level architecture centered around the AppDelegate as the central orchestrator managing all subsystems. Document the modular component design including the observer pattern for system event monitoring, strategy pattern for storage abstraction via UsageStoreProtocol, factory pattern for menu item creation, and singleton pattern for shared resources. Explain the data flow from NSWorkspace application activation events through ActivationMonitor to UsageStore, then to UsageRanker and MenuPresenter for UI display. Detail the integration with macOS frameworks including AppKit for UI, Foundation for core utilities, Spotlight for initial seeding, and NSWorkspace for application monitoring. Include component interaction diagrams showing the relationship between StatusBarController, MenuPresenter, UsageStore, and external system services. Address threading considerations, memory management, and error handling strategies throughout the architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for iTip's data flow architecture. Detail the multi-source data collection pipeline starting with NSWorkspace application activation events processed by ActivationMonitor, combined with periodic network sampling from NetworkTracker. Explain the data transformation pipeline through UsageStore's thread-safe operations, including JSON serialization/deserialization and caching strategies. Document the ranking algorithm implementation in UsageRanker that processes raw usage statistics into display-ready application rankings. Include detailed flow diagrams showing data movement from system events through processing layers to UI presentation. Address data consistency guarantees, conflict resolution strategies, and the temporal aspects of data collection and presentation updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: ActivationMonitor API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the ActivationMonitor component's event handling interface. Document the activation monitoring methods, callback registration patterns, and event subscription mechanisms. Explain the event lifecycle, including activation detection, event firing, and cleanup procedures. Detail method signatures for registering observers, configuring monitoring parameters, and handling activation events. Provide examples of setting up activation monitoring, processing activation events, and managing observer lifecycles. Document thread safety considerations and asynchronous event handling patterns. Include integration examples with NSWorkspace and application activation notifications. Address performance implications, memory management, and best practices for long-running monitoring sessions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Storage & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for iTip's data storage and management system. Detail the UsageStore's thread-safe implementation with JSON serialization, caching strategy, and automatic cleanup of uninstalled applications. Document the UsageRecord data model structure, field definitions, and backward compatibility considerations. Explain the UsageStoreProtocol interface and its role in enabling custom storage implementations. Cover data persistence mechanisms, file I/O operations, and concurrent access handling. Include thread safety measures, atomic operations, and memory management strategies. Document the storage format, data validation rules, and error recovery mechanisms. Provide code examples showing proper usage patterns, custom storage implementations, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: UsageStoreProtocol Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the UsageStoreProtocol interface definition and its role in the application architecture. Explain the protocol's design goals, abstraction benefits, and dependency injection capabilities. Document all protocol methods including load(), save(), and any future extension points. Cover how the protocol enables custom storage implementations, testing strategies with mock objects, and architectural flexibility. Provide examples of protocol conformance, custom implementations, and integration patterns. Address the separation of concerns principle, SOLID design principles, and how the protocol facilitates unit testing and maintainability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for iTip's core functional components. Detail the ActivationMonitor's role in tracking application activation events through NSWorkspace integration, including event handling, memory management, and caching strategies. Document the NetworkTracker's implementation of per-process network usage sampling using system commands, data parsing, and aggregation mechanisms. Explain the UsageStore's thread-safe persistent storage with JSON serialization, caching strategy, and automatic cleanup of uninstalled applications. Cover the MenuPresenter's dynamic menu construction, ranking algorithm implementation, and display formatting. Detail the AppLauncher's application lifecycle management, running application detection, and launch configuration. Document the UsageRanker's priority calculation algorithms and sorting mechanisms. Include implementation details, configuration options, error handling, and performance considerations for each component. Provide code examples demonstrating component usage and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Framework Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document iTip's integration with macOS frameworks and system services. Explain the AppKit integration for menu bar functionality through StatusBarController, Foundation framework usage for core utilities and JSON serialization, and NSWorkspace integration for application monitoring and launching. Detail the Spotlight framework integration for initial data seeding and metadata queries. Document the system permission requirements defined in Info.plist and how the application handles permission changes during runtime. Include the integration with system commands like nettop for network sampling and the handling of system-level events. Address framework-specific considerations including memory management patterns, background task handling, and system resource constraints that impact application design decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: NetworkTracker API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the NetworkTracker component's network monitoring interface. Document the network sampling methods, data collection mechanisms, and performance metrics retrieval. Detail method signatures for starting/stopping sampling, configuring sampling intervals, and accessing collected data. Explain the data structures used for network usage tracking, including byte counts, bandwidth measurements, and process associations. Provide examples of network monitoring setup, data extraction, and performance analysis. Document integration with system networking tools and data parsing mechanisms. Include error handling for monitoring failures, permission issues, and system resource limitations. Address sampling frequency considerations, memory usage optimization, and real-time data processing patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Presentation Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for iTip's presentation layer responsible for menu bar integration and user interface display. Detail the StatusBarController's integration with macOS menu bar system, including delegate implementation, dynamic menu updates, and user interaction handling. Document the MenuPresenter's role in dynamic menu construction, ranking algorithm implementation, and display formatting. Explain the menu item creation process, icon caching mechanisms, and real-time updates. Cover the integration with NSWorkspace for application discovery and the display of application metadata. Include implementation details for menu styling, localization support, and accessibility compliance. Document the relationship between presentation components and data sources, error handling for UI operations, and performance optimization for menu updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model and management documentation for iTip's usage tracking system. Detail the UsageRecord data structure including fields for application identifiers, usage counts, cumulative active duration, last usage timestamps, and network statistics. Document the UsageStore's thread-safe implementation with JSON serialization, file I/O operations, caching mechanisms, and automatic cleanup processes. Explain the SpotlightSeeder's metadata query implementation for initial data seeding, including performance optimization techniques and data transformation processes. Cover data persistence strategies, backward compatibility considerations, and schema evolution. Document the data lifecycle from collection through storage to presentation, including data validation rules, indexing strategies, and query optimization. Address data security considerations, privacy implications, and access control mechanisms. Include practical examples of data manipulation, backup and restore procedures, and troubleshooting common data-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for iTip's application management system. Detail the AppLauncher's implementation of application lifecycle management, including running application detection, launch configuration, and error handling for application launch failures. Document the SpotlightSeeder's metadata query implementation for initial data seeding, including Spotlight framework integration, data transformation processes, and performance optimization techniques. Explain the application discovery mechanisms, running application monitoring, and launch coordination with NSWorkspace. Cover error handling strategies for missing applications, permission issues, and system-level failures. Include implementation details for application state tracking, launch verification, and cleanup procedures. Provide code examples demonstrating proper application management workflows and troubleshooting common launch issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation for iTip's quality assurance approach. Document the unit testing strategy covering individual component testing with mock implementations, property-based testing for data structures, and isolated testing scenarios. Detail the integration testing methodology validating end-to-end workflows, system integration testing, and performance testing under various load conditions. Include guidelines for writing effective tests, mocking external dependencies, and validating component interactions. Address continuous integration testing, automated deployment validation, and quality gates. Provide examples of test scenarios, debugging failed tests, and maintaining test reliability over time.

wiki_catalogs.name: Ranking Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for iTip's application ranking algorithms. Detail the UsageRanker's priority calculation algorithms, including weight assignments for different usage metrics, sorting mechanisms, and ranking criteria. Explain the mathematical models used for determining application priority, including factors such as activation frequency, network usage, and recency weighting. Document the ranking algorithm implementation, performance considerations, and optimization strategies. Cover the relationship between raw usage data and final rankings, including normalization techniques and threshold calculations. Include implementation details for ranking updates, real-time recalculations, and algorithmic adjustments. Provide code examples demonstrating ranking calculations, algorithm customization, and performance tuning for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for iTip's public interfaces and protocols. Document the UsageStoreProtocol defining the contract for storage implementations, including method signatures, parameter specifications, return values, and error handling. Detail the AppLauncher's public interface for application management, including launch methods, error types (AppLaunchError), and configuration options. Document the ActivationMonitor's event handling APIs, callback mechanisms, and subscription patterns. Explain the NetworkTracker's sampling configuration, data retrieval methods, and performance metrics. Include complete method signatures, parameter descriptions, usage examples, and integration patterns for each API component. Address protocol-specific behaviors, thread safety guarantees, and resource management requirements. Provide practical code examples demonstrating API usage, error handling strategies, and best practices for extending or customizing the provided interfaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for contributing to iTip. Document the development environment setup including Xcode configuration, Swift toolchain requirements, and macOS SDK dependencies. Explain the project structure conventions, naming standards, and code organization principles used throughout the codebase. Detail the build and deployment process using xcodebuild, including configuration options, build targets, and release procedures. Document the CI/CD pipeline configuration in GitHub Actions, including automated testing, artifact generation, and release automation. Cover code contribution guidelines, pull request processes, and review criteria. Include debugging techniques, profiling tools, and performance optimization strategies specific to macOS development. Address common development challenges, troubleshooting approaches, and best practices for macOS application development. Provide guidelines for extending functionality, adding new features, and maintaining code quality standards.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# iTip: macOS Menu Bar Application Overview

## Project Introduction

iTip is a macOS menu bar application designed to provide users with insights into their application usage patterns. The application tracks which applications are used most frequently, how long users spend in each application, and the amount of data downloaded through those applications. It presents this information in an accessible menu bar interface, allowing users to quickly identify their most-used applications and launch them directly from the menu bar.

### Purpose Statement
The primary goal of iTip is to help users understand and optimize their macOS application usage by providing real-time analytics and quick access to frequently used applications.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Track application activation events and usage statistics
- Monitor network traffic per application
- Present usage data in a compact, accessible menu bar interface
- Enable quick application launching from the menu bar
- Maintain minimal system resource usage while providing valuable insights

### Target Audience
macOS users who want to understand their application usage patterns, optimize productivity workflows, and quickly access their most-used applications.

## Technical Architecture

The iTip application follows a modular architecture built around several core components that work together to provide comprehensive application usage tracking and presentation.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[AppDelegate] --> B[StatusBarController]
    A --> C[ActivationMonitor]
    A --> D[NetworkTracker]
    A --> E[MenuPresenter]
    A --> F[UsageStore]
    
    C --> G[UsageRecord]
    D --> F
    E --> H[UsageRanker]
    E --> F
    F --> G
    
    style A fill:#e1f5fe
    style B fill:#f3e5f5
    style C fill:#e8f5e8
    style D fill:#fff3e0
    style E fill:#fce4ec
    style F fill:#f1f8e9
```

### Component Breakdown

The application architecture consists of several key layers:

**Presentation Layer**: The `StatusBarController` manages the macOS menu bar integration, while `MenuPresenter` handles the dynamic menu construction and formatting.

**Data Collection Layer**: `ActivationMonitor` tracks application activation events, and `NetworkTracker` monitors per-application network usage through system-level sampling.

**Data Storage Layer**: `UsageStore` provides persistent storage for usage records with thread-safe operations and automatic caching.

**Business Logic Layer**: `UsageRanker` sorts and prioritizes applications based on usage patterns, and `AppLauncher` handles application activation and launching.

### Design Patterns

The application implements several important design patterns:

- **Observer Pattern**: Used extensively for system event monitoring (application activation, network sampling)
- **Strategy Pattern**: `UsageStoreProtocol` enables different storage implementations
- **Factory Pattern**: Menu item creation and configuration
- **Singleton Pattern**: Application-wide access to shared resources

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant StatusBarController
    participant MenuPresenter
    participant UsageStore
    participant UsageRanker
    participant AppLauncher
    
    User->>+StatusBarController: Click Menu Bar Icon
    StatusBarController->>+MenuPresenter: buildMenu()
    MenuPresenter->>+UsageStore: load()
    UsageStore-->>-MenuPresenter: [UsageRecord]
    MenuPresenter->>+UsageRanker: rank(records)
    UsageRanker-->>-MenuPresenter: [UsageRecord]
    MenuPresenter-->>-StatusBarController: NSMenu
    StatusBarController-->>-User: Display Menu
    
    User->>+MenuPresenter: Select App Item
    MenuPresenter->>+AppLauncher: activate(bundleID)
    AppLauncher-->>-MenuPresenter: Result
    MenuPresenter-->>-User: Launch Application
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The application starts through the standard macOS application lifecycle:

**Application Entry Point**: `iTip/main.swift` - Initializes the NSApplication and sets up the AppDelegate as the application delegate.

**Application Delegate**: `iTip/AppDelegate.swift` - Orchestrates the startup sequence, initializes all subsystems, and manages application lifecycle events.

### Core Modules

**Usage Tracking Infrastructure**:
- `iTip/ActivationMonitor.swift` - Monitors application activation events using NSWorkspace notifications
- `iTip/NetworkTracker.swift` - Samples per-process network usage via system commands
- `iTip/UsageStore.swift` - Provides thread-safe persistent storage for usage records
- `iTip/UsageRecord.swift` - Defines the data structure for application usage metrics

**Presentation Layer**:
- `iTip/StatusBarController.swift` - Manages the macOS menu bar integration
- `iTip/MenuPresenter.swift` - Builds dynamic menus with formatted application usage data
- `iTip/UsageRanker.swift` - Ranks applications based on usage patterns

**Application Management**:
- `iTip/AppLauncher.swift` - Handles application activation and launching
- `iTip/SpotlightSeeder.swift` - Pre-populates usage data from Spotlight metadata on cold start

### Configuration Approach

The application uses minimal configuration with sensible defaults:

- **Storage Location**: Uses the standard macOS Application Support directory (`~/Library/Application Support/iTip/usage.json`)
- **Data Persistence**: Thread-safe JSON-based storage with automatic caching
- **System Integration**: Leverages macOS system APIs for application monitoring and menu bar integration

### External Dependencies

The application relies on standard macOS frameworks:

- **Foundation**: Core data structures, JSON serialization, and system utilities
- **AppKit**: User interface components, menu bar integration, and application management
- **OSLog**: System logging infrastructure
- **Spotlight**: Metadata querying for initial application seeding

### Integration Points

**System Integrations**:
- NSWorkspace for application monitoring and launching
- NotificationCenter for system event handling
- Spotlight metadata for initial data population
- System commands (`nettop`) for network traffic sampling

**File System Integration**:
- Application Support directory for persistent storage
- Standard macOS file permissions and sandboxing compliance

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class AppDelegate {
        +StatusBarController? statusBarController
        +ActivationMonitor? activationMonitor
        +NetworkTracker? networkTracker
        +launchApp(sender: NSMenuItem)
    }
    
    class StatusBarController {
        +NSStatusItem? statusItem
        +buildMenu()
        +menuNeedsUpdate(menu: NSMenu)
    }
    
    class MenuPresenter {
        +UsageStoreProtocol store
        +UsageRanker ranker
        +buildMenu() NSMenu
        +attributedTitle(for: UsageRecord) NSAttributedString
    }
    
    class UsageStore {
        +load() [UsageRecord]
        +save([UsageRecord])
        +cachedRecords: [UsageRecord]?
    }
    
    class ActivationMonitor {
        +startMonitoring()
        +stopMonitoring()
        +recordActivation(bundleIdentifier: String, displayName: String)
    }
    
    class NetworkTracker {
        +start(interval: TimeInterval)
        +stop()
        +sample()
    }
    
    AppDelegate --> StatusBarController
    AppDelegate --> ActivationMonitor
    AppDelegate --> NetworkTracker
    StatusBarController --> MenuPresenter
    MenuPresenter --> UsageStore
    MenuPresenter --> UsageRanker
    ActivationMonitor --> UsageStore
    NetworkTracker --> UsageStore
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Application Usage Tracking**:
- Monitors application activation events and maintains detailed usage statistics
- Tracks cumulative foreground active time for each application
- Records application launch counts and timestamps

**Network Usage Analytics**:
- Periodically samples per-process network traffic using system-level tools
- Aggregates downloaded bytes per application over time
- Provides bandwidth usage insights alongside application usage data

**Intelligent Application Ranking**:
- Sorts applications by recency of use and frequency of activation
- Displays top applications in the menu bar for quick access
- Automatically updates rankings as usage patterns change

**Seamless Application Launching**:
- Directly launches applications from the menu bar with one-click access
- Handles both running and non-running applications
- Provides user-friendly error handling for failed launches

### Implementation Highlights

**Performance Optimization**:
- In-memory caching reduces disk I/O during frequent updates
- Debounced periodic saves prevent excessive file system writes
- Asynchronous operations ensure UI responsiveness

**Data Integrity**:
- Atomic file writes prevent corruption during concurrent access
- Automatic cleanup removes references to uninstalled applications
- Backward-compatible data format ensures smooth upgrades

**User Experience**:
- Minimal system resource footprint
- Clean, readable menu formatting with application icons
- Immediate feedback for user interactions

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> ColdStart
    ColdStart --> SpotlightSeed: Empty Store
    ColdStart --> ActiveMonitoring: Has Data
    
    ActiveMonitoring --> ApplicationActivated: User Interaction
    ActiveMonitoring --> NetworkSample: Timer Interval
    ActiveMonitoring --> MenuRequested: User Click
    
    ApplicationActivated --> UpdateUsage: Record Event
    UpdateUsage --> SaveToDisk: Debounce Timer
    SaveToDisk --> ActiveMonitoring: Continue
    
    NetworkSample --> AggregateBytes: Sample Data
    AggregateBytes --> SaveToDisk: Flush Timer
    SaveToDisk --> ActiveMonitoring: Continue
    
    MenuRequested --> BuildMenu: Load & Rank Data
    BuildMenu --> ActiveMonitoring: Menu Closed
    
    ActiveMonitoring --> [*]: Application Termination
```

**Sources:**
- [main.swift](iTip/main.swift)
- [AppDelegate.swift](iTip/AppDelegate.swift)
- [UsageStore.swift](iTip/UsageStore.swift)
- [UsageRecord.swift](iTip/UsageRecord.swift)
- [StatusBarController.swift](iTip/StatusBarController.swift)
- [MenuPresenter.swift](iTip/MenuPresenter.swift)
- [ActivationMonitor.swift](iTip/ActivationMonitor.swift)
- [NetworkTracker.swift](iTip/NetworkTracker.swift)
- [UsageRanker.swift](iTip/UsageRanker.swift)
- [AppLauncher.swift](iTip/AppLauncher.swift)
- [SpotlightSeeder.swift](iTip/SpotlightSeeder.swift)
- [IntegrationTests.swift](iTipTests/IntegrationTests.swift)
- [build.yml](.github/workflows/build.yml)

</blog>

wiki_repo.name: iTip
wiki_repo.optimized_catalog:
".
